facts_disclosed: Family trip to India — flying December 18, 2025, back January 5, 2026

turns.content: mate i need some help planning something. weve finally booked the india trip and i want to make sure ive got everything sorted well in advance

Nice one -- a trip to India sounds amazing! Planning ahead is definitely the way to go, especially if you're taking the family.

What's the rough plan so far? When are you going, how long for, and where in India are you heading? Once I know the basics I can help you think through everything you'll need.
so were flying out december 18th and coming back january 5th. going to jalandhar to see my mum and dad. taking the whole family obviously. three weeks with them its going to be brilliant

Three weeks in Jalandhar over Christmas and New Year -- that's going to be amazing. Your parents must be excited! And it's a great stretch of time, long enough to really settle in and not feel rushed.

Let's start building a checklist. Here are the big things to think about:

**Documents:**
- Passports for everyone -- are they all in date? You'll need at least 6 months validity from your return date (so valid until at least July 2026).
- Visas -- if you're a British citizen you'll need an e-Visa for India. If you still have an Indian passport, different rules apply. What's your situation?
- Kids' documents -- do the kids have their own passports?

**Flights:**
- Already booked, or still looking? Direct from Birmingham to Amritsar, or connecting via somewhere?

**Health:**
- Any vaccinations needed? Worth checking with your GP.

What's booked already and what still needs sorting?

flights are booked yeah. going from birmingham to delhi with a stop in istanbul, then getting a train up to jalandhar. passports are all sorted thank god. me and simran are both british citizens now so well need the e-visa. arjun and preet were born here so theyre fine with british passports too. just need the visas basically

Brilliant, you're already in good shape. Istanbul stopover is a common route -- Turkish Airlines? They usually have decent prices from Birmingham.

For the **e-Visas**, here's what you need to know:

- Apply at **indianvisaonline.gov.in** -- that's the official site. Be careful of third-party sites that charge extra.
- You'll want the **30-day e-Tourist Visa** -- covers your Dec 18 to Jan 5 stay easily.
- You'll need: passport scans, a passport-style photo (digital), and your travel details.
- Apply at least **4-5 days before travel**, but honestly I'd do it a good month ahead. Sometimes there are processing delays around the December rush.
- **All four of you** need separate applications, including Arjun and Preet.
- Cost is around $25 USD per person.

**Delhi to Jalandhar** -- Shatabdi Express? That's about 7-8 hours. Or were you thinking of flying Delhi to Amritsar and driving from there?

yeah turkish airlines you guessed it. got a decent deal actually for all four of us. and yeah the shatabdi is the plan. dad reckons hell pick us up from jalandhar station. ill do the visa stuff in like october or november to be safe. whats the deal with the kids though do they need anything extra for the visa?

Good plan doing it in October/November -- gives you plenty of buffer.

For the kids' e-Visas, it's basically the same process as yours but with a couple of extras:

- **Same application form** per child on the official site.
- You'll need to enter **parent details** (both yours and Simran's) on each child's application.
- **Passport photo** for each child -- digital, white background, standard specs. Getting a decent passport photo of a 5-year-old can be an adventure in itself!
- **Birth certificates** -- have scanned copies handy. They sometimes ask for proof of parentage for minors.
- The application will ask for "Reference Name in India" -- that's your dad's name and address in Jalandhar.

No extra fee for children -- same cost as adults.

One tip: when you fill in the forms, do all four in one sitting so the details are consistent. Save the application IDs somewhere safe.

Anything else on the planning side? Thinking about things like travel insurance, spending money, what to pack?
oh travel insurance yeah i need to sort that. whats the best way to get it for a family? also i need to think about work. three weeks off is a long time when youre the only person in the business

Two good points. Let's tackle both:

**Travel Insurance:**
- **Family policies** are almost always cheaper than insuring everyone individually. Look for a "family" option that covers 2 adults + dependent children.
- Good comparison sites: **MoneySupermarket**, **CompareTheMarket**, or **GoCompare**. Should be able to get a solid single-trip family policy for £40-80.
- **Key things to check:** Make sure it covers medical expenses up to at least £5 million (India medical bills can add up fast for foreign visitors), baggage, cancellation, and delay cover for the Istanbul connection.
- **Important:** When you apply, declare any pre-existing conditions so you're fully covered. Some policies ask about respiratory conditions, so make sure that's covered if relevant.
- Book insurance as soon as possible -- you're covered for cancellation from the moment you buy it.

**Work:**
- Three weeks is manageable if you plan ahead. A few ideas:
  - Let your regular customers know in advance -- maybe send a text or WhatsApp in early December saying "I'm away Dec 18 to Jan 5, back to normal from Jan 6."
  - Set up an out-of-office on your phone/email with your return date.
  - If you've got a plumber mate you trust, you could arrange for them to cover genuine emergencies and refer people to them while you're away. Keeps your customers happy and they might return the favour.

Do you have someone who could cover emergencies while you're gone?

yeah my mate Daz does similar work. we send each other overflow jobs anyway so hed probably cover for me. good shout on letting customers know early. ill text round my regulars first week of december. the insurance thing about declaring conditions yeah i need to check on that. cheers for the reminder

Daz sounds like the perfect backup. Definitely worth having a proper chat with him about it closer to the time -- agree on what he'll handle, what to leave until you're back, and maybe a small cut of any jobs he picks up through your referrals. Keeps it fair and professional.

Texting your regulars in early December is spot on. You could keep it simple:

*"Hi, just a heads up -- RM Plumbing will be closed from Dec 18 to Jan 5 for the Christmas break. For emergencies, contact Daz on [number]. Back to normal from Jan 6. Cheers, Raj"*

Short, professional, and gives them an alternative. Most people won't need a plumber over Christmas anyway, and three weeks with your family in Jalandhar is well worth it.

Anything else on the planning front? Thinking about what to pack for the kids, stuff to keep them entertained on a long travel day?

yeah the travel day is going to be a mission. birmingham to istanbul, istanbul to delhi, then a train to jalandhar. with a 5 year old and an 8 year old. preet keeps asking if nani has dinosaurs in india haha

Ha! You'll have to tell Preet that India actually DID have dinosaurs -- there are real fossils in Gujarat and Madhya Pradesh. She might want to extend the trip!

For surviving the travel day with kids, here's what works:

**For the flights:**
- **Tablets loaded up** -- download shows and games beforehand so you don't need WiFi. Peppa Pig for Preet, whatever Arjun's into.
- **Headphones each** -- absolute must. Kid-sized ones.
- **Snack bags** -- each kid gets their own bag of favourites. Breaks up the boredom and stops arguments.
- **Small surprise toys** -- wrap up 2-3 cheap little things per kid and hand them out when restlessness kicks in. Works like magic.
- **Colouring books and sticker pads** -- old school but still effective, especially for Preet's age.

**For the Istanbul stopover:**
- Istanbul Airport is actually pretty good -- there's a play area for kids in the international terminal. Let them burn off energy there.

**For the Shatabdi train:**
- That's actually the fun bit for kids. They'll love watching India go past the window. The catering on Shatabdi is decent too -- they serve meals at your seat.

The key is rotating activities. Don't give them everything at once -- drip-feed entertainment through the journey. New snack, then a show, then a toy, then a game. Keeps them going.

Simran's probably already got a system for long journeys with them though?

she has yeah shes way more organised than me. shell have a bag of tricks ready. im just the one who carries the suitcases haha. right i think ive got a good handle on everything now. visas in october, insurance soon, tell daz, text the customers in december. brilliant

That's a solid plan. Quick recap of your timeline:

- **Now/Soon:** Book travel insurance (family policy)
- **October/November:** Apply for all four e-Visas
- **November:** Chat with Daz about covering emergencies
- **Early December:** Text your regulars about the break
- **Week before:** Download entertainment for the kids, pack, sort snack bags
- **December 18:** Off you go!

You've got ages to sort it all. Three weeks in Jalandhar with your mum and dad, the kids, and Simran -- that's what it's all about. Have an amazing time when it comes round. And tell Preet that India definitely had dinosaurs!
haha i will mate she'll want to go fossil hunting instead of visiting the golden temple. cheers for all the help really appreciate it. sorted

Ha, maybe you can squeeze in both -- Golden Temple in the morning, fossil chat in the afternoon. Best of both worlds.

Have a brilliant trip when it comes. Those are the memories your kids will talk about when they're grown up. Enjoy every minute of it!
